channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Opinion: The immorality of betting on war
item.description: Traders on prediction markets bet on nearly anything. One made more than half a million dollars betting on the U.S. strike against Iran. But should people wager on human suffering?
item.content:encoded:
Traders on prediction markets bet on nearly anything. One made more than half a million dollars betting on the U.S. strike against Iran. But should people wager on human suffering?

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Curling had its moment at the Olympics and now Paralympics. It sparked a curling bonanza in America
item.description: Hundreds of people become interested in curling every four years and the 2026 numbers already show that boom.
item.content:encoded:
Hundreds of people become interested in curling every four years and the 2026 numbers already show that boom.

(Image credit: Claire Harbage)
item.dc:creator: Anusha Mathur

item.title: Too hyped up to sleep? Rituals to calm your body and mind before bed
item.description: The chaos of the day can make it hard to shut off your brain and fall asleep. Here's how to create a relaxing environment before bedtime to help you quiet the chatter and feel ready for sleep.
item.content:encoded:
The chaos of the day can make it hard to shut off your brain and fall asleep. Here's how to create a relaxing environment before bedtime to help you quiet the chatter and feel ready for sleep.

(Image credit: Photo illustration by Becky Harlan/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Clare Marie Schneider

item.title: One week into the Iran war, the fallout is global
item.description: The war is no longer just about the U.S., Israel and Iran. More countries are getting caught in the political crossfire or being drawn into the fighting themselves.
item.content:encoded: The war is no longer just about the U.S., Israel and Iran. More countries are getting caught in the political crossfire or being drawn into the fighting themselves.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Iran's president apologizes for strikes on neighbors as strikes pound their cities
item.description: President Masoud Pezeshkian said Saturday that a demand by the U.S. for an unconditional surrender is a "dream that they should take to their grave." He also apologized for Iran's attacks on regional countries.
item.content:encoded:
President Masoud Pezeshkian said Saturday that a demand by the U.S. for an unconditional surrender is a "dream that they should take to their grave." He also apologized for Iran's attacks on regional countries.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: What the Trump administration says about why it went to war with Iran
item.description: The Trump administration says it is "laser focused" and mission driven, but the messaging has been varied. The range of cited motivations for striking Iran now are sometimes at odds with each other.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration says it is "laser focused" and mission driven, but the messaging has been varied. The range of cited motivations for striking Iran now are sometimes at odds with each other.

(Image credit: Andrew Caballero-Reynolds/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Trump looks to turn attention to Western Hemisphere at Americas summit
item.description: President Trump is set to gather with Latin American leaders on Saturday at his Miami-area golf club as his administration looks to turn attention to the Western Hemisphere, at least for a moment.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump is set to gather with Latin American leaders on Saturday at his Miami-area golf club as his administration looks to turn attention to the Western Hemisphere, at least for a moment.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump administration's embattled FDA vaccine chief is leaving for the second time
item.description: The FDA's controversial vaccine chief, Dr. Vinay Prasad, is leaving the agency. It's the second time he has abruptly departed following decisions involving the review of vaccinations and specialty drugs.
item.content:encoded:
The FDA's controversial vaccine chief, Dr. Vinay Prasad, is leaving the agency. It's the second time he has abruptly departed following decisions involving the review of vaccinations and specialty drugs.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Family, former presidents and a Hall of Famer give Rev. Jesse Jackson a final sendoff
item.description: Several speakers at Jackson's funeral invoked his hallmark catchphrases: "Keep hope alive" and "I am somebody."
item.content:encoded:
Several speakers at Jackson's funeral invoked his hallmark catchphrases: "Keep hope alive" and "I am somebody."

(Image credit: Erin Hooley)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Bernard LaFayette, Selma voting rights organizer, dies at 85
item.description: Bernard LaFayette, who died Thursday, laid the foundations of the Selma, Alabama, campaign that culminated in the passage of the Voting Rights Act. He was a Freedom Rider and helped found the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee.
item.content:encoded:
Bernard LaFayette, who died Thursday, laid the foundations of the Selma, Alabama, campaign that culminated in the passage of the Voting Rights Act. He was a Freedom Rider and helped found the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee.

(Image credit: Gregory Smith)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
